insert_settings.description:
Settings applied to INSERT operations only (both bulk Arrow IPC and
parameterized `INSERT VALUES`).

Typical use: enable server-side batching via `async_insert` to reduce
part creation when many small or concurrent writes hit the same tables.

CodeBackfillSweepConfig.description: Cadence for the coverage-driven code-backfill sweep.

cron.description:
Cron expression with seconds field (6-field: `sec min hour dom mon dow`).
When absent, the task runs on a default 60-second interval.

cross_file_resolve_timeout_ms.description:
Wall-clock budget for the sequential cross-file resolution phase
(import edges, call edges). 0 = no timeout.

fetch_concurrency.description:
Maximum concurrent Gitaly repository fetch operations. Controls how
many repositories can be downloaded simultaneously in the pipelined
code indexer. 0 = no limit. Defaults to 6.

job_timeout_secs.description: Hard wall-clock budget (seconds) for one repository job (fetch + index); exceeding it aborts and retries. Keep below `nats.ack_wait_secs`. 0 = no timeout. Defaults to 250.

max_total_bytes.description:
Total discovered bytes above which a repository is skipped entirely
(indexed empty, then checkpointed). Bounds pathologically large repos.
0 = no limit. Defaults to 10 GB.

per_file_timeout_ms.description:
Global per-file resolution timeout in milliseconds.
Applied to all languages unless the language's own DSL rules
specify a different value. 0 = no global timeout.

write_channel_capacity.description: In-flight batches the streaming sink holds before back-pressuring the parser. Defaults to 8.

write_max_concurrent_writes.description: Concurrent in-flight inserts from the streaming sink. Defaults to 8.

write_slice_rows.description: Rows per ClickHouse insert from the streaming sink; large tables are sliced to this. Defaults to 500000.

DatalakeRetryConfig.description:
Tuning for the SDLC datalake extract retry loop.

The first attempt uses the datalake's configured `max_block_size`
(typically `datalake_batch_size`). After a failure, subsequent attempts
seed at `halving_initial_block_size` and halve on each retry, with
`halving_min_block_size` as the floor.

halving_initial_block_size.description:
Starting `max_block_size` (in rows) for the halving series after the
first failure. Sized to stay safely under the Arrow String int32
offset cap even on unexpectedly heavy text columns.

halving_min_block_size.description:
Floor for the halving series. Prevents pathologically tiny scans
after repeated retries.

EngineConfiguration.description:
ETL engine configuration.

# Defaults

- `max_concurrent_workers`: 16
- `concurrency_groups`: empty
- `topics`: empty (no retry/DLQ by default)
- `handlers`: defaults for all handlers
- `modules`: all variants of [`IndexerModule`] (universal indexer)

concurrency_groups.description:
Named concurrency groups with their limits.
Subscriptions reference these by name via `SubscriptionConfig::concurrency_group`.

datalake_retry.description: Datalake retry tuning shared by all SDLC pipelines.

handlers.description: Per-handler domain configuration (batch sizes, pipeline settings).

max_concurrent_workers.description: Maximum concurrent message handlers across all modules. Defaults to 16.

modules.description:
Modules whose handlers this indexer process should register. Defaults to all
modules for backward compatibility (universal indexer). Set to a subset to
run a specialised pool, e.g. `[code]` for a code-only Deployment.

topics.description:
Per-subscription message processing policy (retry, concurrency, DLQ).
Keyed by a human-readable label matching topic name constants.

partition_min_rows.description: Skip partitioning a scope smaller than this; the probe isn't worth it.

stream_block_size.description:
Rows per block streamed from the datalake (`max_block_size`). Larger blocks
amortize per-batch write round-trips (more throughput) at the cost of peak
memory per in-flight block.

system_notes_resolve_lookup_batch_size.description: Maximum number of items bound into each SystemNote resolver lookup.

FeatureScope.description:
A feature flag scoped to root namespaces: `enabled` toggles it, an empty
`namespaces` list means every namespace, otherwise only those ids.

FeaturesConfig.description:
An unrecognized key fails deserialization as an unknown [`Feature`] variant,
rather than being silently ignored.

GlobalDispatcherConfig.description:
Per-task schedule configuration.

Each scheduled task embeds this via `#[serde(flatten)]` in its own typed config struct.
The scheduler reads it via `task.schedule()`.

cron.description:
Cron expression with seconds field (6-field: `sec min hour dom mon dow`).
When absent, the task runs on a default 60-second interval.

HandlersConfiguration.description:
Typed per-handler domain configuration (batch sizes, pipeline settings).

Engine-level config (retry, concurrency, DLQ) lives in `topics`.

IndexerModule.description:
Indexer module selector. Each variant maps to a domain in `crates/indexer/src/modules/`.

An indexer process registers handlers only for the modules listed in
[`EngineConfiguration::modules`], letting operators run multiple specialised
indexer Deployments (e.g. a light SDLC pool and a beefy code pool) from the
same binary.

oneOf.description: SDLC entity handlers (one per ontology entity type, subscribing to global or namespace topics).
Code indexing handler: clones repositories, runs tree-sitter, writes the code graph.

MigrationCompletionConfig.description:
Per-task schedule configuration.

Each scheduled task embeds this via `#[serde(flatten)]` in its own typed config struct.
The scheduler reads it via `task.schedule()`.

cron.description:
Cron expression with seconds field (6-field: `sec min hour dom mon dow`).
When absent, the task runs on a default 60-second interval.

NamespaceDeletionSchedulerConfig.description:
Per-task schedule configuration.

Each scheduled task embeds this via `#[serde(flatten)]` in its own typed config struct.
The scheduler reads it via `task.schedule()`.

cron.description:
Cron expression with seconds field (6-field: `sec min hour dom mon dow`).
When absent, the task runs on a default 60-second interval.

NamespaceDispatcherConfig.description:
Per-task schedule configuration.

Each scheduled task embeds this via `#[serde(flatten)]` in its own typed config struct.
The scheduler reads it via `task.schedule()`.

cron.description:
Cron expression with seconds field (6-field: `sec min hour dom mon dow`).
When absent, the task runs on a default 60-second interval.

NamespaceTarget.description: A namespace with lists of deployments and/or statefulsets to monitor.

NatsConfiguration.description:
NATS connection settings.

Matches siphon's QueuingConfig fields.

ack_wait_secs.description: Acknowledgment wait time in seconds before message redelivery. Defaults to 300.

auto_create_streams.description: Whether to auto-create streams on startup. Defaults to true.

batch_size.description:
How many messages to fetch per batch. Higher values improve throughput
but increase memory usage. Defaults to 10.

connection_timeout_secs.description: Connection timeout in seconds. Defaults to 10.

consumer_inactive_threshold_secs.description:
Inactive threshold for versioned dispatch consumers in seconds.
After this duration with no activity, NATS auto-deletes the consumer.
Used for Siphon dispatch consumers during blue-green deployments.
Clamped to a minimum of 60 seconds. Defaults to 3600 (1 hour).

consumer_name.description:
Consumer name for durable subscriptions. Defaults to None (ephemeral consumer).

**Ephemeral consumers** (`None`): Created on subscribe, destroyed on disconnect.
Messages are only delivered while connected. Good for transient workers or testing.

**Durable consumers** (`Some("name")`): Persist across restarts. NATS tracks the
last acknowledged message, so reconnecting consumers resume where they left off.
Required for reliable message processing.

For horizontal scaling, give all instances the same `consumer_name`. NATS will
distribute messages across them (each message delivered to exactly one instance).

fetch_expires_secs.description:
Server-side timeout in seconds for `consume_pending` batch fetch.
Must be long enough for the NATS server to scan through gaps between
matching messages in filtered consumers. Defaults to 5.

max_deliver.description: Maximum redelivery attempts. None means unlimited. Defaults to 5.

password.description:
Optional password for authentication.
For production, prefer environment variables over storing in config files.

request_timeout_secs.description: Request timeout in seconds. Defaults to 5.

stream_max_age_secs.description: Maximum age of messages in seconds before deletion. Defaults to 14400 (4 hours).

stream_max_bytes.description: Maximum bytes per stream before oldest messages are deleted. Defaults to None (unlimited).

stream_max_messages.description: Maximum messages per stream. Defaults to None (unlimited).

stream_replicas.description:
Number of stream replicas for fault tolerance. Defaults to 1.
Production should use 3 for fault tolerance.

subscription_buffer_size.description:
How many messages to buffer per subscription. Defaults to 100.

This controls the capacity of the internal channel between the NATS fetch loop
and your message handler. When `subscribe()` is called, a background task fetches
messages and queues them in this buffer.

- **Smaller buffer**: Less memory, but the fetch loop may block waiting for the handler
- **Larger buffer**: More messages pre-fetched, smoother throughput, higher memory usage

For slow handlers or bursty workloads, consider increasing this value.

tls_ca_cert_path.description:
Path to CA certificate (PEM) for verifying the NATS server.
Setting this enables TLS (connection uses `tls://` scheme).

tls_cert_path.description:
Path to client certificate (PEM) for mTLS authentication.
Must be paired with `tls_key_path`.

tls_key_path.description:
Path to client private key (PEM) for mTLS authentication.
Must be paired with `tls_cert_path`.

graph_query_cache_enabled.description:
NATS KV cache for graph query results (webserver).
Excluded from ClickHouse SETTINGS (app-level only).

graph_query_cache_ttl.description:
Graph query cache TTL in seconds.
Excluded from ClickHouse SETTINGS (app-level only).

max_ast_depth.description:
ClickHouse `max_ast_depth`. Limits AST nesting depth.
Default: 10,000 (10x ClickHouse default of 1,000).

max_ast_elements.description:
ClickHouse `max_ast_elements`. Limits AST node count after parsing.
Default: 1,000,000 (20x ClickHouse default, matches Siphon).

max_bytes_to_read.description:
ClickHouse `max_bytes_to_read` in bytes. Limits uncompressed data
read from tables. Unset by default.

max_expanded_ast_elements.description:
ClickHouse `max_expanded_ast_elements`. Limits AST node count after
alias/macro expansion. Default: 10,000,000 (20x ClickHouse default).

max_memory_usage.description:
ClickHouse `max_memory_usage` in bytes. Limits the amount of RAM
a single query can consume on the ClickHouse server. When exceeded,
ClickHouse aborts the query with MEMORY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED. Unset by
default (ClickHouse uses its server-level setting).

max_rows_in_set.description:
ClickHouse `max_rows_in_set`. Limits the size of hash tables built
for IN (subquery) clauses. Unset by default.

max_rows_to_read.description:
ClickHouse `max_rows_to_read`. Limits the total number of rows
read from tables during query execution. Unset by default.

QuerySettings.description:
Top-level query settings loaded from YAML. Contains a `default` config
and optional per-query-type overrides keyed by snake_case query type
name (e.g. `traversal`, `aggregation`, `search`).

```yaml
query:
  default:
    max_execution_time: 30
    use_query_cache: false
    query_cache_ttl: 60
  aggregation:
    max_execution_time: 60
```

ScheduledTasksConfiguration.description: Typed per-task configuration for all registered scheduled tasks.

SchemaConfig.description: Schema configuration: version retention and related settings.

indexer_schema_wait_timeout_secs.description:
How long the indexer waits for the dispatcher to prepare its schema
version before exiting non-zero (relying on the orchestrator to restart
it). The indexer retries with backoff within this budget. Must be at
least 1.

max_retained_versions.description:
Number of schema version table-sets to retain.

After migrating to version N, the indexer keeps the N active tables plus
the N-1 rollback target. Must be at least 2.

version_poll_interval_secs.description:
How often the webserver polls `gkg_schema_version` for the active
version, in seconds. Must be at least 1.

SiphonRouterConfig.description:
Drives the continuous Siphon CDC trigger: which JetStream the orchestrator
drains and how many messages per `consume_pending` call.

StaleEdgeReconciliationConfig.description:
Tombstones stale FK-derived "latest"/single-value edges whose endpoint no
longer matches the owner node's current FK column. ReplacingMergeTree keys
the edge on its (mutable) `target_id`, so an FK change orphans the old edge
instead of replacing it; this sweep reconciles them off the indexing path.

cron.description:
Cron expression with seconds field (6-field: `sec min hour dom mon dow`).
When absent, the task runs on a default 60-second interval.

SubscriptionConfig.description:
Per-subscription message processing policy (retry, concurrency, DLQ).

Lives under `engine.topics.<name>` in YAML. Applied to the `Subscription`
at handler registration time, so all handlers sharing a subscription
share the same processing policy.

Retries are opt-in: a subscription with no retry config will ack on failure.

concurrency_group.description:
Which concurrency group this subscription belongs to.
Maps to a named semaphore in `EngineConfiguration::concurrency_groups`.

dead_letter_on_exhaustion.description: Route exhausted retries to the dead letter queue.

max_ack_pending.description:
Per-consumer cap on simultaneously-delivered-but-not-yet-acked messages.
When absent, the NATS server default applies (currently 1000).

max_attempts.description:
Maximum total attempts (including the first delivery) before giving up.

`max_attempts: 1` means the message is processed once with no retries.
`max_attempts: 5` means 1 initial attempt + 4 retries.

When absent, failures are acked immediately (retries are opt-in).

retry_interval_secs.description:
Delay in seconds between retry attempts. Used as the NATS nack delay.
When absent, nacks use immediate redelivery.

TableCleanupConfig.description:
Per-task schedule configuration.

Each scheduled task embeds this via `#[serde(flatten)]` in its own typed config struct.
The scheduler reads it via `task.schedule()`.

cron.description:
Cron expression with seconds field (6-field: `sec min hour dom mon dow`).
When absent, the task runs on a default 60-second interval.
